page: Amazon Web Services c5ad.24xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor and AMD EPYC 7R32 processors. This x86_64 architecture server features 96 virtual CPUs across 48 physical cores with simultaneous multithreading enabled, operating at a clock speed of 3.3 GHz. The processor includes 16 MB of L3 cache, 0.5 MB of L2 cache, and 32 KB of L1d cache. It supports AVX, AES, and SHA_NI cryptographic instructions, though it lacks AVX-512 capabilities. The instance is equipped with 192.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core.

For local storage, the c5ad.24xlarge includes 3800 GB of NVMe SSD instance store volumes, which are directly bundled with the hardware profile. Networking capabilities feature a baseline bandwidth of 20 Gbps. In benchmark evaluations, the server demonstrates strong performance in cryptographic operations, ranking in the top tier for PassMark CPU encryption. It also achieves strong results in PassMark CPU score, extended instructions, physics tests, and memory score. Multi-core performance is generally average to strong, with notable efficiency in software compilation, ray tracing, and image processing. However, single-core performance is relatively weak, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance falling into the bottom 10%.

This hardware configuration offers a balanced cost-efficiency profile for compute-intensive workloads that require high-speed local storage. The combination of 96 dedicated vCPUs and 3800 GB of NVMe SSD storage makes it highly suitable for multi-threaded applications, software compilation, image processing, and high-throughput database operations like Redis. It is less suited for workloads heavily dependent on single-threaded performance or AVX-512 instruction sets.
description: Amazon Web Services c5ad.24xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 7R32 processor running at 3.3 GHz. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, this x86_64 server includes 192.0 GB of DDR4 memory and 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. It delivers 20 Gbps of baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark data highlights strong multi-core capabilities, particularly in cryptographic encryption, software compilation, and ray tracing, though single-core performance remains a relative bottleneck. With its high core count and fast local instance storage, this server is well-suited for multi-threaded workloads, software development pipelines, and high-throughput database operations.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c5ad.24xlarge is a compute-optimized server with 96 vCPUs, 192 GB DDR4 RAM, and 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, delivering strong multi-core and cryptographic performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c5ad.24xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 96 vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and 3800 GB NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A high-core compute-optimized instance featuring fast local NVMe SSD storage and strong multi-threaded performance for demanding workloads.
bullet_points: 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores powered by AMD EPYC 7R32 at 3.3 GHz
192 GB of DDR4 memory with 2.0 GB per core resource density
3800 GB of bundled local NVMe SSD instance storage
Top-tier performance in cryptographic encryption benchmarks
20 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor
Best suited for software compilation, multi-threaded tasks, and database operations
